description: Inclusion status assigned by the public v0 release rules.
Peer-reviewed research, peer-reviewed contextual comparator, commercial contextual comparator, or excluded internal row.
Frontend hint for whether the point should be visible by default.
Short label for legends/tooltips.
Publication/source quality: peer-reviewed journal, DOI-verified source, commercial/spec-sheet, or internal/unverified.
Whether the plotted value was extracted directly from a source table/figure in this pipeline or imported from a peer-reviewed secondary/meta-analysis dataset.
Clean public-facing sample label; raw workbook IDs such as `(39)` are not used as primary public titles.
Public wording explaining how to interpret the source.
Required citation instruction for use of plotted values.
True only for DOI-verified CNT/graphene research records.
True for non-CNT comparator/reference materials.
True when commercial, MatWeb, manufacturer, or mixed commercial provenance is involved.
True when the row comes from a peer-reviewed secondary/meta-analysis workbook with original DOI resolved but values not independently re-extracted in this pipeline.
True when method, atmosphere, gauge length, strain rate, or other strict-comparison metadata are missing.
True when a raw unit/scale was inferred and should be visibly reviewed before final official release.
True when form factor is not fiber_yarn; cross-form plots must be visually flagged.
True when the row could become strict-comparison eligible after condition metadata are curated.
True only when strict-comparison requirements are currently met.
True when normalized/Ashby-style comparison is defensible with visible source flags.
True for all records included in public v0 exploratory plots.
Measurement-level flag for density/specific-property metrics used in normalized comparisons.
Measurement-level flag mirroring strict_comparison_ready.
Measurement-level flag for normalized metrics on normalized-eligible records.
Measurement-level flag for v0 exploratory plotting.
Measurement-level warning text for inferred units or non-primary benchmark provenance.
Semicolon-delimited reason a source record was excluded from public v0.
Citation metadata for a peer-reviewed compilation source such as the Bulmer/James meta-analysis workbook.
Higher-priority public/internal record that appears to duplicate this secondary row.
Canonicalized duplicate group metadata used to prevent duplicate source rows from plotting as independent scientific points.
Record ID selected as the canonical plotted representative of a duplicate group.
